inputs.userGoal: Deploy a microservices-based e-commerce backend with independent catalog, order, and payment services, use AKS with Helm for deployments, and implement service mesh for traffic control

reasoning.whyChosen: Central resource organization following CAF naming conventions. East US provides good latency for North American customers and has comprehensive Azure service availability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: East US selected for optimal cost-performance balance and proximity to target market

resources.name: vnet-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Provides network isolation and security boundaries for microservices. CNI integration with AKS requires dedicated subnet.
reasoning.tradeoffs: /16 provides ample room for growth while maintaining simplicity

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the resource group

resources.name: crecommerceprod001

reasoning.whyChosen: Premium SKU provides geo-replication, private link, zone redundancy, and customer-managed keys for production workloads. Managed identity integration eliminates credential management.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Premium costs more but provides enterprise-grade features required for production microservices

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the resource group

resources.name: aks-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Standard SKU provides 99.95% SLA. Separate system and user node pools for workload isolation. Azure CNI with Cilium provides advanced networking and security. Zone redundancy ensures high availability.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier balances cost and SLA requirements. Cilium provides eBPF-based networking for better performance

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires VNet subnet for Azure CNI networking integration

resources.name: sqlsrv-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized SQL Server for all microservice databases with AAD integration for secure access management.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Single server reduces management overhead while maintaining logical separation via databases

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the resource group

resources.name: sqldb-catalog-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Serverless General Purpose provides cost-effective scaling for catalog microservice with automatic pause/resume capabilities.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Serverless has cold start latency but provides significant cost savings during low usage periods

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the SQL Server

resources.name: sqldb-orders-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Provisioned General Purpose for consistent performance needed for order processing transactions.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Provisioned ensures consistent performance for transactional workloads

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the SQL Server

resources.name: sqldb-payments-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Business Critical tier provides read replicas and enhanced security for sensitive payment data processing.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Higher cost justified by security and compliance requirements for payment processing

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the SQL Server

resources.name: redis-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Premium Redis provides persistence, clustering, and VNet integration for session state and caching across microservices.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Premium costs more but provides enterprise features needed for production caching

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the resource group

resources.name: kv-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Centralized secrets management for database connection strings, API keys, and certificates used by microservices.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Standard tier sufficient for software-protected keys while maintaining security best practices

resources.dependencyReasoning: Must be created within the resource group

resources.name: appgw-ecommerce-prod-001

reasoning.whyChosen: Application Gateway v2 provides L7 load balancing, SSL termination, and WAF protection for incoming traffic to microservices.
reasoning.tradeoffs: Regional solution provides lower latency than Front Door while offering advanced L7 features

resources.dependencyReasoning: Requires dedicated subnet in VNet for deployment

overallReasoning.summary: Architecture implements a microservices pattern on AKS with Istio service mesh for advanced traffic management, security, and observability. Each microservice (catalog, orders, payments) has dedicated databases optimized for their specific requirements. Container Registry provides secure image storage, while Redis enables distributed caching and session state. Key Vault centralizes secrets management, and Application Gateway provides secure ingress with WAF protection.
overallReasoning.tradeoffs: Premium/Standard tiers selected balance production requirements with cost efficiency. Istio service mesh adds complexity but provides essential microservices capabilities like mTLS, traffic splitting, and observability. Single SQL Server reduces management overhead while maintaining logical separation via databases.

plan.validation: All resources are properly sized for production workloads with high availability across availability zones. Service mesh integration provides the traffic control and security required for microservices communication. Helm deployment strategy supported through AKS native integration.
